title: Anxiety drives child mental health referrals past one million
description: Demand is soaring beyond capacity, meaning children in England wait years for help with various conditions.
content:
The number of children referred to mental health services in England has risen by 10% in a year to more than one million, according to a report.
The latest data from 2024-2025 is almost double the number in 2018-19.
Anxiety was listed as the most com... [2271 chars]

title: Man due in court charged with attempted murder after car hit pedestrians
description: The man has been remanded in custody following a collision in Ealing, west London, on Saturday.
content:
A man has been charged with attempted murder after a vehicle hit pedestrians on a busy west London road on Saturday.
Timir Ahmed Mohamed, 34, is charged with five counts of attempted murder as well as dangerous driving, failing to stop, failing to pr... [501 chars]

title: White working-class children 'failed by schools system'
description: The inquiry spoke to thousands of young people and their parents, as well as hundreds of teachers.
content:
Once-in-a-generation reforms are needed to fix an education system that "is not serving the interests of white working-class children", an independent inquiry has concluded.
It was set up last summer to look into why such children are the lowest-perf... [3323 chars]

title: UK weather: Temperatures to fall but relief from the heat may be short-lived
description: Many will experience cooler air and some rain this week before a possible return to hot weather at the weekend.
content:
The UK is set for cooler weather this week after a spell of record-breaking heat, but the reprieve may not last long.
New June heat records were set on three consecutive days last week, ending with 37.3C at Santon Downham in Suffolk, smashing the pre... [2062 chars]

title: Pause HS2 reset until you are confident it can be delivered, NAO tells ministers
description: Spending watchdog says high-speed rail project must be put on stable footing to avoid repeat of costly past failures
content:
Revised plans for HS2 should not be put into action until the government is confident they can be delivered, according to the public spending watchdog.
